ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee record */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the employee is located */,
  "education_level" INTEGER /* Educational background of the employee */,
  "job_experience" INTEGER /* Whether the employee has job experience */,
  "job_training_required" INTEGER /* Whether the employee requires job training */,
  "no_of_employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees with this profile */,
  "region_of_employment" INTEGER /* Region of employment */,
  "prevailing_wage" REAL /* Prevailing wage for the job */,
  "unit_of_wage" TEXT /* Unit of wage (e.g., Year, Month) */,
  "full_time_position" INTEGER /* Whether the position is full-time */,
  "case_status" INTEGER /* Status of the employee case */,
  "company_age" INTEGER /* Age of the company in years */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race or ethnicity of the employee */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry in which the employee works */,
  "date_of_hire" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "date_of_termination" TEXT /* Date the employee left the company, if applicable */,
  "employee_status" TEXT /* Current status of the employee (e.g., active, terminated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_full_time_position FOREIGN KEY ("full_time_position") REFERENCES full_time_positions ("position_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_training_required FOREIGN KEY ("job_training_required") REFERENCES job_training ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_education_level FOREIGN KEY ("education_level") REFERENCES educational_levels ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_region_of_employment FOREIGN KEY ("region_of_employment") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_case_status FOREIGN KEY ("case_status") REFERENCES employment_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_experience FOREIGN KEY ("job_experience") REFERENCES job_experience ("experience_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "educational_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level (e.g., High School, Master's) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  "degree_type" TEXT /* Type of degree (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, Ph.D.) */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study (e.g., Computer Science, Business Administration) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_experience" (
  "experience_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for job experience */,
  "experience_description" TEXT /* Description of job experience level (e.g., Y, N) */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Number of years of job experience */,
  "previous_employer" TEXT /* Name of the previous employer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experience_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_training" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for training requirements */,
  "training_required" TEXT /* Indicates if training is required (Y/N) */,
  "training_type" TEXT /* Type of training required (e.g., technical, soft skills) */,
  "training_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the required training */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent the region belongs to */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country within the region */,
  "city" TEXT /* City within the region */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wages" (
  "wage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for wage record */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the wage applies */,
  "prevailing_wage" REAL /* Prevailing wage in the region */,
  "unit_of_wage" TEXT /* Unit of wage (e.g., Year, Month) */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency in which the wage is paid */,
  "minimum_wage" REAL /* Minimum wage in the region */,
  "maximum_wage" REAL /* Maximum wage in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wages_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "company_age" INTEGER /* Age of the company in years */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry in which the company operates */,
  "company_size" TEXT /* Size of the company (e.g., small, medium, large) */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Annual revenue of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the employment status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the employment status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "termination_reason" TEXT /* Reason for termination (if applicable) */,
  "rehire_eligibility" TEXT /* Whether the employee is eligible for rehire (Y/N) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "full_time_positions" (
  "position_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for position */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the full-time position (Y/N) */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Number of hours per week for the position */,
  "flexibility" TEXT /* Flexibility of the position (e.g., remote, on-site, hybrid) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("position_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the employee data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the device used to access the data */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
